data.body: When an OpenRouter model calls a tool that doesn't exist (e.g. kno_next), local-worker.ts runTool() returns {ok: false, content: 'Unknown tool kno_next'} which is sent back as a tool_result. The model gets the error but the session still ends with exit code 1. The executeLease loop at openrouter-session-runtime.ts:184-249 should continue normally after an unknown tool (it does via 'continue' at line 245), so the fatal exit is elsewhere — likely in completeIteration or the take loop condition check. Unknown tool calls must be non-fatal; agents constantly call nonexistent tools and should just get the error response and continue working. Evidence: session term-1772797553131-nson3u (knot foolery-2234) exited code 1 after 'Unknown tool kno_next' response. The empty console was because the error was only in stderr (184 chars) with no stdout context.
data.title: Unknown tool calls from OpenRouter models should not be fatal
